content:
Prevent XSS, CSRF, Authentication Issues, and Common Security Vulnerabilities Before They Become Serious Problems

6 min read
Just now

--

Security is one of the most important aspects of modern web development, yet it’s often treated as an afterthought. Many developers focus on building features quickly and only think about security after an application is deployed. Unfortunately, that’s exactly when attackers start looking for weaknesses.
Press enter or click to view image in full size

source-internetIf Not a Member READ FROM HERE
The good news is that Angular was designed with security in mind. It includes several built-in protections that help developers create secure applications without relying entirely on third-party libraries. However, these built-in features are only effective if you understand how they work and avoid practices that bypass them.
In this guide, we’ll explore the most common security threats in Angular applications and learn practical ways to prevent them.
Why Security Matters in Angular Applications
Every Angular application interacts with user data in some way. Whether it’s login credentials, payment information, or personal details, attackers are always searching for…
